message:
fix(btree): allocate page buffers at full page_size, not usable_size (#46)

Stock SQLite expects every B-tree page to be exactly page_size bytes on
disk. When reserved_bytes > 0, usable_size < page_size, and our balance
code was allocating undersized buffers. This caused PRAGMA integrity_check
to report "database disk image is malformed" when stock SQLite opened
FrankenSQLite-authored files.

Changes:
- build_page: allocate vec![0u8; page_size], place cells from usable_size
- balance_deeper: allocate new root at page_size
- balance_quick: allocate new sibling at page_size
- BtCursor: added page_size field to thread full size through balance calls
- All balance functions receive page_size parameter

Cell content layout still uses usable_size (correct per SQLite spec),
leaving the reserved region as zero bytes at the end of each page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
